Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC154 describes decomposing a requirement and sending a decompose event; it does not mention DOORS or any 3rd-party requirements management tool or assert storage/management of requirements in such a tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC155 covers refining a requirement and related event messaging but contains no reference to DOORS or to managing requirements in a 3rd-party tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC156 describes modifying a requirement and sending a modify event; there is no mention of DOORS or managing requirements in a 3rd-party tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC158 addresses merging requirements and event messages; it does not reference DOORS or any 3rd-party requirements management tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: The test explicitly shows the system polling DOORS for requirement information, which demonstrates integration with the DOORS tool and therefore behavior consistent with requirements being managed in a 3rd-party tool. Confidence is partial because the test demonstrates interaction with DOORS for a specific event flow but does not assert all possible behaviors, boundaries, or negative paths related to 'requirements shall be managed' (e.g., CRUD operations, error handling, or confirmation that all requirements reside exclusively in DOORS).

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: Test TC141 concerns establishing a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement by a registered subscriber. It does not assert management of non-requirement artifacts in 3rd-party tools nor integration with Rational Rose or ClearCase.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: Test TC142 describes a non-registered subscriber attempting to establish a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement; it does not mention managing non-requirement artifacts or any interaction with 3rd-party tools (Rational Rose, ClearCase). No assertions relate to storing or managing artifacts in external tools.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: Test TC154 describes decomposing a requirement and temporary links; no mention of non-requirement artifact management or integration with specified third-party tools.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: Test TC155 concerns refining a requirement and link creation; it does not cover management of non-requirement artifacts or interactions with Rational Rose/ClearCase.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: Test TC157 covers deleting a requirement and resulting link status changes; it does not address handling or management of non-requirement artifacts in third-party tools.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: Test TC162 describes event server constructing composite messages and polling DOORS for requirement info. Although it mentions an external tool (DOORS), it addresses requirement-related data aggregation, not management of non-requirement artifacts in tools like Rational Rose or ClearCase.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: Test TC165 addresses user processing of event messages and updating links; it does not reference managing non-requirement artifacts or using third-party tools for artifact management.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test (TC141) directly asserts the positive half of the requirement: a registered subscriber is able to establish a trace. It verifies permission for registered subscribers but does not by itself demonstrate enforcement against non-registered subscribers.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test (TC142) directly asserts the negative half of the requirement: when the subscriber is not registered, an attempt to establish a trace results in no trace being established. It verifies prohibition but does not by itself assert that registered subscribers can establish traces (the positive permission).

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: TC143 verifies that a subscriber can register with a subscriber manager (registration flow). This is supportive infrastructure for the requirement but does not itself verify that only registered subscribers may establish traces.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: TC144 describes failing to complete registration and removal from the system; it does not assert trace establishment behavior or enforcement of trace creation rules.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: TC146 concerns subscriber manager registration with an event server; it does not assert or verify trace-establishment permissions tied to subscriber registration.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: TC149 concerns event notification storage for registered subscribers and does not assert any behavior about establishing traces or restricting trace establishment to registered subscribers.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: This test addresses behavior of a non-registered subscriber attempting to establish a trace; it verifies that non-registered users cannot perform a trace, but does not verify that a user can register as a subscriber.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: This test explicitly exercises and asserts successful registration: the steps state the subscriber registers itself and the postcondition asserts the subscriber is registered under the subscriber manager, directly matching the requirement that a user shall register as a subscriber. The requirement is simple and the test covers the happy path implied.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: This test describes a failure/exit from the registration process and asserts removal from the system. It is topically related to registration but does not assert successful registration, so it does not verify the requirement that a user shall register as a subscriber.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: This test covers Subscriber Manager registration with an event server, which is unrelated to a user registering as a subscriber; no assertion verifies the user registration requirement.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: This test concerns publishing events in real time and subscriber manager subscriptions; it does not assert or exercise a user registering as a subscriber.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: This test concerns event notification delivery and storage for registered subscribers; it assumes subscribers exist but does not exercise or assert the act of a user registering as a subscriber.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test explicitly performs the registration action ('The subscriber registers itself with the subscriber manager') and asserts the postcondition that the subscriber is registered under the subscriber manager. This directly matches the requirement's mandated behaviour (happy path) including the outcome, so it fully verifies the requirement.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test explicitly exercises the negative path where the subscriber does not register itself under a subscriber manager and asserts the resulting system behavior (removal). It does not verify the positive behaviour 'on registration the subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager', but it is relevant to error/negative handling of registration, therefore partially covers requirement-related behavior.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test concerns the subscriber manager registering with an event server, not the subscriber's registration under a subscriber manager. No assertion in the test exercises or verifies subscriber registration behavior.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test addresses event publication and subscriber manager subscriptions; it does not include actions or assertions that a subscriber registers itself under a subscriber manager, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test focuses on event notification storage by a subscriber manager for registered subscribers. It presumes registration but contains no steps or assertions that perform or verify subscriber registration under a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test assumes the subscriber manager already has registered subscribers and tests event notification processing. It does not exercise or assert the subscriber registration action or outcome, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: The test explicitly instructs a subscriber to establish a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement and states the postcondition that a trace is established. This directly implements the core requirement: creating a trace between an artifact and a requirement (semantic equivalence to establishing a subscription from an artifact to the requirement). Both action and success postcondition are present.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This negative test verifies behavior when an unregistered subscriber attempts to create a trace (subscription). It exercises error handling/permission aspects relevant to establishing subscriptions but does not demonstrate successful subscription establishment; therefore it partially covers the requirement by validating a related negative scenario.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This test shows that subscriber managers can be subscribed to requirements and that events are delivered to subscribers, which demonstrates the existence and use of subscriptions. However, it does not show the explicit act of establishing a subscription from an artifact to a requirement (the creation step), so it only partially verifies the requirement.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This test demonstrates establishing links between requirements (refinements). While it shows trace/link creation, it concerns requirement-to-requirement links rather than traces between requirements and other artifact types or subscriptions originating from an artifact. Thus it partially overlaps with the concept of trace establishment but does not fully satisfy the requirement's artifact-subscription dimension.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: This test describes event composition and identifying related requirements for subscribers. It references subscribers and requirements linked to artifacts but does not assert any action that establishes a trace/subscription from an artifact to a requirement. It is topically related but does not verify establishment of subscriptions/traces.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test directly asserts that a subscriber is registered under a subscriber manager (maps to 'placed under the control'). However, it exercises only the subscriber artifact type and only the positive registration path; it does not demonstrate that every artifact type is placed under control nor shows enforcement for all artifacts, boundaries, or persistence. Therefore partial coverage.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test exercises a negative/error path related to registration with a subscriber manager: it shows the system's behavior when a subscriber does not become registered (removed). It provides coverage for error handling for subscriber artifacts, but does not prove the broader requirement that each artifact (all artifact types) shall be placed under control, nor demonstrate mechanisms ensuring placement for all artifacts. Thus partial coverage for the requirement.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test verifies that a subscriber manager registers with the event server. It does not assert that artifacts are placed under the control of a subscriber manager, so it does not provide evidence for the requirement.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test concerns publishing an event and that a subscriber manager is subscribed to a requirement and receives a notification. It does not assert that an artifact is placed under control of a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: This test shows the event server constructing composite messages by identifying requirements linked to the same artifact and sending messages to subscribers. It does not assert that the artifact is placed under the control of a subscriber manager, nor that placement is enforced for all artifacts.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: Test concerns ordering of event message processing by a subscriber manager. It references a subscriber manager receiving messages but contains no assertion that artifacts are placed under the control of a subscriber manager; it does not verify placement or registration of artifacts.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: This test covers subscriber registration, not deletion of subscriptions; no delete action or postcondition related to subscriptions is present.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: This test describes a subscriber failing to register and being removed; it does not exercise or assert deletion of subscriptions by the user.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: The test explicitly exercises the user deleting a subscription and states the postcondition that the subscription has been deleted, directly satisfying the requirement's observable behavior.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: This test addresses publishing events to subscriber managers and does not include steps or assertions about deleting subscriptions.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: This test concerns deleting a requirement and associated link/status behavior; it does not mention subscriptions or deleting subscriptions.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC143 concerns a subscriber registering with a subscriber manager, not the subscriber manager registering with the eventserver; it does not exercise or assert the manager-to-eventserver registration behavior.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC146 explicitly asserts that the subscriber manager registers its IP, port, and name with the event server and postcondition states the manager is registered. This directly exercises the registration action in the requirement, but the test does not demonstrate the consequential receipt of relevant event messages or error/negative paths, so it only partially covers the full requirement intent.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC148 verifies that an offline subscriber manager can retrieve missed events when coming online. It shows receipt after reconnection but does not assert the initial registration of the subscriber manager with the eventserver.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC149 verifies that a subscriber manager can receive and store an event for a registered subscriber, but it does not assert that the subscriber manager registers itself with the eventserver. The precondition refers to subscribers registered under the manager, not the manager's registration with the eventserver.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC150 focuses on receipt and processing of speculative event notifications; it assumes local subscriber registration and does not assert that the subscriber manager registers itself with the eventserver.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: TC161 demonstrates that the event server holds and uses information (subscriber manager identity, IP and port) to send notifications and that subscriber managers receive event messages. This addresses the requirement purpose (receiving relevant event messages) but does not explicitly show the manager performing the registration action with the eventserver; thus it partially supports the requirement when combined with an explicit registration test.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: The test explicitly sets the subscriber manager online in Preconditions, names the scenario 'An event is published in real time', and the Steps state 'An event notification is sent to the relevant subscriber manager.' Together these cover the online condition, the server sending the notification (push behavior), and the real-time intent expressed in the test title.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test describes the subscriber manager coming online and downloading missed events that were stored while it was offline (pull/download). It exercises offline-to-online recovery (pull) rather than the push-in-real-time behavior required when the manager is online.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: The test documents that an event notification is sent and received by the subscriber manager and stored locally, which aligns with push delivery. However it lacks an explicit precondition stating the manager is online and contains no timing/assertion that delivery was 'in real time' or automated assertions verifying the push modality, so it only partially verifies the requirement.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test shows the subscriber manager receives and processes an event notification (covers delivery aspect). It does not explicitly assert the 'online' precondition nor explicitly assert 'real time' delivery or provide automated checks for timing or push-vs-pull behavior, so coverage is partial.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reason: This test explicitly describes the event server building and sending notification messages to subscriber managers (push behavior) and a postcondition that the message is received. It therefore verifies the push delivery behavior; combined with the test context this satisfies the requirement's core elements.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC146 addresses registration of the subscriber manager with the event server (IP/port/name). Registration is a different behaviour than pulling outstanding messages on initial startup and does not assert retrieval of stored notifications.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC147 describes real-time publishing of an event to an online subscriber manager; it does not cover the initial offline-to-online transition and pulling outstanding messages.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: The test (TC148) explicitly exercises the scenario where the subscriber manager is offline while the event server has stored undelivered notifications, the subscriber manager then 'comes online' and the postcondition asserts it 'has successfully downloaded the relevant event notifications from the event server.' This directly implements the requirement's behaviour (initial startup triggers retrieval of outstanding messages).

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC149 describes receiving a non-speculative event and storing it in the local event log; it does not describe the initial pull-from-server behaviour when the subscriber manager first comes online.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC150 covers receipt and processing of a speculative event notification while the manager is already registered/online; it does not exercise the initial 'comes on line' pull of outstanding events.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reason: TC164 addresses ordering of processing of received events; it does not reference initial startup behaviour or pulling outstanding messages from the event server.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test describes a real-time publication scenario where the requirement changes and an event notification is sent to the relevant subscriber manager that subscribed to the requirement, directly demonstrating receipt for managed artifacts.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test verifies that when a subscriber manager comes online it downloads stored event notifications intended for it, demonstrating receipt of notifications on behalf of managed artifacts in a reconnect/recovery scenario.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: The test explicitly sends an event to the subscriber manager for a registered subscriber and asserts that the relevant subscriber manager receives the event notification (and stores it). This directly exercises the requirement's core behavior (receipt of notifications on behalf of managed artifacts).

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: This test describes sending a speculative event to a subscriber manager for a registered subscriber and asserts that the relevant subscriber manager receives and processes it, directly confirming receipt on behalf of managed artifacts.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: The test traces the event server identifying subscriber managers and sending notifications; the postcondition asserts receipt by each relevant subscriber manager on behalf of its managed subscribers, matching the requirement's scope and action.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reason: The test focuses on display ordering of events once messages exist (precondition: messages received for an artifact) but does not assert that the subscriber manager received those notifications on behalf of its managed artifacts nor exercise the receipt mechanism; therefore it does not provide direct evidence of the requirement.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test asserts the core happy-path action of creating/establishing a trace between an artifact and a requirement, directly demonstrating core trace management capability. It does not show retrieval, editing, deletion, persistence, or multi-artifact management, so coverage is partial.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test verifies a negative authorization scenario for trace creation: when a non-registered subscriber attempts to establish a trace, no trace is created. That demonstrates enforcement of trace management policy (preventing unauthorized trace creation) but does not exercise other management functions (storage, retrieval, update, multi-artifact management).

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test verifies subscriber registration with the subscriber manager (postcondition: subscriber is registered). Registration is a prerequisite for trace management but does not itself assert any trace management behavior, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test covers the subscriber manager registering with an event server (network/registration behavior). It does not assert any trace management behavior for artifacts, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.
Reason: This test verifies event notification receipt and storage in an event log. While relevant to subscriber manager operation, it does not assert trace creation, storage, retrieval, or other trace-management behaviors required by RQ112.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: This test documents receipt of a non-speculative (i.e., evolutionary) event and a different handling outcome (stored in local event log) compared to speculative handling described elsewhere. That implies different behavior depending on event type, which supports the differentiation requirement, but it does not explicitly assert that the subscriber manager parsed the message and decided 'evolutionary' vs 'speculative'; so confidence is partial.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: The test names a speculative notification and shows receipt and generic processing, but it does not assert that the subscriber manager parses the message to determine its type or that differentiation logic is executed. Evidence is topical but does not verify differentiation behavior.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: This test explicitly states the subscriber manager 'parses the event message to determine that it is a speculative event' and then executes speculative-specific processing. That demonstrates detection and handling of speculative events (one side of the required differentiation). It does not, however, explicitly assert detection of evolutionary events or show an explicit branching test that distinguishes both types; therefore coverage of the full differentiation requirement is partial.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: This test describes the event server sending evolutionary messages and the subscriber manager receiving them, but it does not assert that the subscriber manager parses the message to determine it is evolutionary or that it explicitly differentiates evolutionary from speculative events. It documents receipt but not the subscriber manager's decision logic, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.
Reason: This test focuses on event server-side construction of composite messages for speculative events and on sending to subscribers. It does not exercise or assert the subscriber manager's logic for differentiating event types, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC146 describes subscriber manager registration with the event server and does not include any assertion about storing evolutionary events in an eventlog.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC148 asserts the subscriber manager downloads missed event notifications from the event server when coming online, but does not explicitly assert that those downloaded notifications are stored in the subscriber manager's eventlog. The storage behavior is not explicitly stated.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC149 explicitly states the postcondition that the event notification message is stored in the local event log for a non-speculative event notification. Semantically, 'non-speculative event' corresponds to an evolutionary event; the test directly asserts storage in the subscriber manager's local event log, satisfying the requirement's core behavior.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC150 covers receipt and processing of a speculative event notification. Speculative events are distinct from evolutionary events required by RQ114; no assertion about storing evolutionary events in an eventlog is present.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC152 covers processing of speculative event messages by the subscriber manager (speculative, not evolutionary). It does not assert storing evolutionary events in an eventlog.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC159 documents the event server storing events in its event log and categorizing events as evolutionary or speculative, but it does not assert that the subscriber manager stores evolutionary events in its own eventlog.

Description: RQ114 The subscriber manager shall store information for evolutionary events in an eventlog.
Reason: TC161 describes notifying subscriber managers that an evolutionary event occurred and that the message is received by subscriber managers; it does not assert that the subscriber manager stores the event in an eventlog. No explicit storage assertion is present.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC150 verifies that a subscriber manager receives speculative event notifications. It tests message receipt/processing but does not describe user actions or GUI construction of speculative queries.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC152 describes processing of a speculative event by a subscriber manager and assumes a speculative query has been issued, but it does not state who issued the query, nor does it describe any GUI-based user action to construct the speculative query. No assertions in this test verify user GUI construction behavior.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC154 covers decomposing a requirement and sending a decompose event; it does not address user construction of speculative queries using a GUI or verify any GUI mechanisms for composing such queries.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC155 addresses the user refining a requirement and the triggering of a refine event. Although it involves a user initiating an event, it does not state or verify that the user constructs speculative queries via a GUI; it covers refinement behavior rather than GUI-based speculative query composition.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC156 concerns the user modifying a requirement and sending a modify event; it does not describe constructing speculative queries through a GUI or verify GUI controls or behaviors for speculative query construction.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC162 concerns the event server constructing composite event messages from related requirements and polling DOORS for quantitative information. It does not describe a user constructing a speculative query in a GUI or verify GUI behaviors. No direct evidence of the user's GUI-based construction capability.

Description: RQ115 The user shall be able to construct speculative queries using a GUI.
Reason: TC165 describes a user processing event messages and selecting tentative links. While this captures user interaction, it pertains to reviewing and applying event messages, not to constructing speculative queries via a GUI. There is no assertion that the user composes speculative queries or uses GUI controls to do so.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: This test concerns non-speculative event notifications and storing messages in the local event log. It does not address speculative events nor any immediacy requirement, so it does not verify RQ116.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: The test documents reception and processing of an event notification (and is labeled as 'speculative' in the test title). It shows the subscriber manager processes notifications, but it does not show or assert immediacy (no timing or immediate-processing guarantee). Thus it partially aligns with the requirement's functional aspect but lacks evidence for 'immediately'.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: This test explicitly exercises processing of a speculative event by the subscriber manager (detection, model injection, re-execution, collating results, restoring state and responding). However, it does not assert or measure 'immediately' (no timing, latency, or urgency guarantee). Therefore the core behavior is verified but the immediacy attribute is not evidenced.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: This test concerns the event server constructing composite messages for a speculative event (server-side behavior). It does not show that the subscriber manager immediately processes speculative events, so it does not verify RQ116.

Description: RQ116 The subscriber manager shall immediately process speculative events.
Reason: This test exercises user attempts to process events in incorrect order and enforcement of ordering. It does not address speculative-event processing or the immediacy requirement.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test verifies subscriber registration; it does not reference model re-execution or speculative value injection.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test concerns receipt/storage of a non-speculative event notification and does not mention injecting speculative values or re-executing the model.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test only verifies that the subscriber manager receives a speculative event notification; it does not state that the model is injected with speculative values or re-executed.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: The test explicitly describes that the subscriber manager injects values (speculative inputs) into the performance model and re-executes it, then collates and returns results and restores state. This directly implements the requirement's action and input.

Description: RQ117 The subscriber manager shall re execute the model using the speculative values.
Reason: This test concerns the event server constructing and sending composite speculative event messages to subscribers; it does not assert that the subscriber manager will inject speculative values and re-execute its model.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC147 describes publication of an event and notification delivery. It does not verify that the subscriber manager reports impact of speculative changes on other requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC149 covers receiving and storing non-speculative event notifications. It does not address speculative processing or reporting whether a speculative change negatively impacts other requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC150 describes receipt and processing of a speculative event notification but only asserts that the message is processed according to protocols. It does not state that the subscriber manager reports whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC152 explicitly exercises the subscriber manager processing a speculative event, re-executing a model, collating results and returning results. This demonstrates 'reporting results' but does not explicitly assert that the results indicate whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints (no explicit negative-impact determination, comparison to other requirements/constraints, or explicit reporting of impact outcome).

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC156 concerns modifying a requirement and link status changes. It does not assert that the subscriber manager reports whether a speculative change negatively impacts other requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ118 The subscriber manager shall report results indicating whether the speculative change negatively impacts other requirements and constraints.
Reason: TC162 demonstrates the event server assembling related requirement constraints into the message sent to subscribers. However, this is evidence about the event server's message construction, not an assertion that the subscriber manager assesses or reports whether a speculative change negatively impacts other requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC143 covers subscriber registration with the subscriber manager; it does not address restoring an executable model.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC144 addresses subscriber registration failure and removal; it contains no steps or postconditions about model execution or restoration.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC146 addresses registration of the subscriber manager with the event server; it does not reference model execution or restoration.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC148 concerns coming online and downloading missed events; it does not mention executing or restoring an executable model.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC149 describes receipt and storage of non-speculative event notifications; it does not mention modifying or restoring a performance/executable model.

Description: RQ119 The subscriber manager shall restore the executable model to its prior state.
Reason: TC152 explicitly states that the subscriber manager 'restores the model to its prior state' and the postcondition reiterates that 'The performance model is restored to its original state', directly matching the requirement's core behavior. However the test is a high-level test description and does not enumerate error paths, concurrency, or boundary conditions, so confidence is partial.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The test explicitly lists the driver running as a precondition and expects 'A new event message is sent.' This aligns with the requirement conceptually (driver + event), but there is no explicit verification step that the driver triggered the event versus some other mechanism, nor are error/negative scenarios covered.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The test explicitly requires 'The trigger driver is running' (shows the driver is part of the scenario) and states 'A decompose event message is sent' (shows an event is produced). However the test text does not contain an explicit assertion or step that verifies the driver itself triggered the event (no check that the driver API was invoked or that the driver produced the message). Therefore it partially supports the requirement but does not fully verify driver utilization.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The precondition names the trigger driver as running and the postcondition states 'A refine event message is sent', indicating events occur while driver runs. The test description does not include an explicit verification that the driver component triggered the event (no driver-call assertions or failure/negative checks).

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: Precondition asserts the trigger driver is running and postcondition states 'A modify event message is sent', showing the test expects events while the driver is running. The description lacks explicit assertions that the driver component performed the trigger or validation of driver behavior; thus partial coverage.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.
Reason: The precondition and postcondition indicate the presence of a running trigger driver and that an event message is sent, which semantically aligns with using a driver to trigger events. There is no explicit verification that the driver triggered the event or tests of driver failure modes.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: This test explicitly names and exercises an 'Add New Requirement' event and asserts that 'The user adds a new requirement' and that an event message is sent. This directly satisfies the requirement that event types include adding new requirements.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: The refine test demonstrates creation of an additional requirement ("adds an additional requirement related to it") but does so under a 'refine' event type rather than an explicit 'add new requirement' event. This provides partial, relevant evidence that events can result in adding requirements, but it does not by itself prove presence of the specific 'add new requirement' event type.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: This test exercises a 'modify' event (changing an existing requirement) and does not assert adding new requirements or the presence of an 'add' event type.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: This test covers a 'delete' event (marking a requirement obsolete) and does not assert adding new requirements or the presence of an 'add' event type.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: This test covers a 'merge' event (merging requirements) and does not assert adding new requirements or the presence of an explicit 'add new requirement' event type.

Description: RQ121 Event types shall include adding new requirements.
Reason: This test concerns constructing composite event messages for a 'speculate' event and does not assert creation of new requirements or an 'add' event type.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: The test explicitly names a 'decompose' event and describes the user selecting an existing requirement and decomposing it into two or more requirements, matching the requirement text directly. Post-conditions assert a decompose event message is sent and the requirement is decomposed into two or more parts, satisfying the required behavior.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: This test covers a 'refine' event (adding related requirements) rather than decomposing one requirement into two or more. It does not demonstrate the specific decomposing behavior required by RQ122.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: This test covers a 'delete' event (marking a requirement obsolete). It does not involve decomposing a requirement into multiple requirements and thus does not verify RQ122.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: This test covers a 'merge' event (merging two or more requirements). It does not assert or mention decomposing a requirement into two or more requirements, so it does not verify RQ122.

Description: RQ122 Event types shall include decomposing one requirement into two or more requirements.
Reason: This test concerns constructing composite event messages for a 'speculate' event with polling and message customization. It does not assert a decomposing event type or splitting a requirement into multiple requirements, so it does not verify RQ122.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test describes adding a new requirement as a standalone new event, not refining an existing requirement by adding a related requirement; it does not verify the refine behavior.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test covers a 'decompose' event (splitting a requirement), which is a different operation than refining an existing requirement by adding an additional requirement; it does not assert the refine behavior.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: The test explicitly exercises a 'refine' event, the selection of an existing requirement, and the addition of at least one related requirement; its postcondition asserts that the requirement is refined. These directly satisfy the requirement's intent.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test covers modifying an existing requirement, which differs from refining by adding an additional related requirement; it does not assert the refine event behavior.

Description: RQ123 Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reason: This test covers merging requirements, which is not the same as refining one requirement by adding another; it does not verify the refine behavior.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: This test exercises an 'add new requirement' event; it does not assert or verify modification of an existing requirement.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: This test exercises a 'decompose event' (decompose a requirement) which is a different event type; it does not assert or verify a 'modify' event.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: This test exercises a 'refine event' (refine a requirement) rather than a 'modify' event; it does not assert or verify modification event behavior required by RQ124.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: This test explicitly exercises a 'modify event', verifies the requirement is changed, and that a modify event message is sent — directly satisfying the requirement that event types include modifying a requirement.

Description: RQ124 Event types shall include modifying a requirement.
Reason: This test exercises a 'delete event' (delete a requirement) and checks obsolescence and delete-event messaging; it does not assert or verify a 'modify' event.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test covers a 'new' (add requirement) event and does not mention or assert any delete/delete-event behavior.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test covers a 'decompose' event and does not mention or assert any delete/delete-event behavior.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test covers a 'refine' event (adding related requirements) and does not mention or assert any delete/delete-event behavior.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: This test covers a 'modify' event (changing a requirement) and does not mention or assert any delete/delete-event behavior.

Description: RQ125 Event types shall include deleting a requirement.
Reason: The test explicitly names and exercises a 'delete' event for a requirement and asserts post-conditions that a delete event message is sent and requirement status becomes obsolete. This directly satisfies the requirement that event types include deleting a requirement.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: TC153 covers adding a new requirement and sending a new event message; it does not include merging behavior or a merge event type.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: TC154 describes a 'decompose' event (splitting a requirement), which is the opposite of merging and does not verify that event types include merging requirements.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: TC155 addresses 'refine' events that add related requirements; it does not assert merging requirements or a merge event type.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: TC156 covers a 'modify' event for changing a requirement; it does not reference merging behavior.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: TC158 explicitly defines a 'merge' event, steps to select two or more requirements, and post-conditions stating the requirements are merged and a merge event message is sent with link reassignment. These assertions directly and semantically satisfy the requirement that event types include merging requirements.

Description: RQ126 Event types shall include merging requirements.
Reason: TC162 describes constructing composite event messages for a speculate event and polling for related requirements; it does not assert a merge event or merging of requirements.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test shows that a requirement change causes an event notification to be sent (publisher side). It does not assert that the eventserver receives the event message, so it is partial evidence for the requirement.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test documents that adding a requirement causes a new event message to be sent. It verifies the sender behavior but contains no assertion that the eventserver receives that message, so coverage is partial.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test demonstrates that changing a requirement causes a modify event message to be sent (i.e., the trigger side of the requirement). It does not, however, assert that the eventserver receives that message, so it only partially addresses the requirement.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test directly exercises the event server receiving an event message and processing it (receive, parse, categorize, store). However, it does not explicitly state that the trigger was a requirement change; therefore it verifies the receive behavior but not the 'when a requirement changes' trigger, so coverage is partial.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test assumes as a precondition that the event server has received an event message; it does not demonstrate that receipt occurs as a result of a requirement change nor exercise the receive logic. It is topically related but does not directly verify the requirement end-to-end.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: Like TC160, this test assumes the event server has already received an event message (a speculative one). It covers receipt as a precondition but does not show receipt triggered by a requirement change or explicitly exercise the receive behavior.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: Although this test asserts that a subscriber manager stores the event notification message in a local event log, the requirement specifically requires the eventserver to record the event in an eventlog. The actor differs (subscriber manager vs eventserver), so this does not satisfy the requirement.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: The test explicitly states that the event server stores event details in its event log and that the postcondition is that the event is stored in the log. This directly and fully exercises the requirement: upon receipt the eventserver records the event in an eventlog.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: This test focuses on identifying subscribers from a received event and searching for subscribers; it does not assert that the eventserver records the event in an event log.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: This test focuses on notifying subscribers for evolutionary events and sending notifications; it does not assert that the eventserver records the event in an event log.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.
Reason: This test details constructing and sending composite event messages for speculative events; it does not assert that the eventserver records the received event in an event log.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: TC147 describes end-to-end publishing and that notifications are sent to subscriber managers; it does not include steps or assertions that the eventserver identifies all relevant subscribers upon receipt of the event.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: TC149 verifies that a subscriber manager receives and logs notifications for registered subscribers; it does not exercise or assert the eventserver's act of identifying relevant subscribers when an event is received.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: TC160 explicitly exercises the receipt of an event and asserts steps where the event server identifies impacted requirements, searches for subscribers, and the postcondition states 'All relevant subscribers are identified.' This directly implements and verifies the requirement's core behavior and its completeness outcome.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: TC161 assumes 'The event server has identified a list of subscribers' as a precondition and focuses on notifying subscriber managers. It does not exercise or assert the identification action itself; therefore it provides topical relevance but not independent verification of identification completeness or error handling.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.
Reason: TC162 similarly treats identification as a precondition and exercises subsequent per-subscriber processing and message construction. It does not independently verify the initial identification step or completeness, so it only partially supports the requirement conceptually.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: This test describes receipt/processing by the subscriber manager but does not demonstrate that the eventserver differentiates between evolutionary and speculative events.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: This test documents the subscriber manager determining that a message is speculative. It verifies classification behavior, but in the subscriber manager component, not the eventserver; it does not provide evidence that the eventserver differentiates event types.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: The test explicitly instructs the event server to categorize each event as speculative or evolutionary and the postcondition reiterates that the event is categorized accordingly. This is a direct, explicit verification of the requirement.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: This test assumes the event server has identified an event as evolutionary (uses an "evolutionary event message" as a precondition) and exercises the evolutionary path (notification). It provides indirect evidence that evolutionary events are treated distinctly but does not explicitly assert the classification step within the eventserver.

Description: RQ130 The eventserver shall differentiate between evolutionary and speculative events.
Reason: This test exercises handling specific to speculative events (constructing composite messages for a speculative event) and therefore supports that speculative events are handled differently. However, it does not explicitly assert that the eventserver performs the classification step itself.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test covers the real-time publication path where an event notification is sent to a subscriber manager when a requirement changes. It demonstrates the sending action but does not explicitly state the event is an 'evolutionary' event, so it provides partial support for the requirement.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test demonstrates that a subscriber manager receives event notifications and logs them for non-speculative events. While it shows the receiving/logging part of notification flow, it does not explicitly assert that the eventserver sends notifications specifically for evolutionary events, so it only partially supports the requirement.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test verifies identification/categorization of an event as evolutionary, which is a necessary precursor to notifying subscribers. However it does not assert that the eventserver issues notifications to subscribers when the event is categorized as evolutionary, so it only partially supports the requirement.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test explicitly names 'evolutionary event' and details steps where the event server builds and sends a notification to each relevant subscriber manager and asserts the postcondition that the event message is received by each relevant subscriber manager. That directly exercises and verifies the requirement's core behavior (detect evolutionary event -> notify subscribers).

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.
Reason: This test concerns speculative events specifically and demonstrates message construction and sending for speculative events. It does not address evolutionary events; therefore it does not provide direct evidence that evolutionary events trigger subscriber notifications.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC149 concerns non-speculative event notifications and storing messages in a local log. It does not address speculative events or the construction of composite event messages and therefore provides no verification for RQ132.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC150 shows that subscriber manager receives speculative event notifications. This addresses delivery to subscribers (a downstream step) but does not verify that the eventserver constructed a composite event message, so it is only weakly relevant.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC152 verifies downstream handling of a speculative event (subscriber manager parsing, model injection/execution, response). It confirms that speculative messages are processed by subscribers but does not assert that the eventserver constructed a composite event message. Thus it is related but does not directly verify the requirement.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: TC159 demonstrates the eventserver receiving, parsing, and categorizing events as speculative. These are prerequisite behaviors relevant to constructing a composite message, but the test does not assert that the eventserver constructs a composite event message; therefore it provides partial (supporting) evidence only.

Description: RQ132 For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reason: The test TC162 explicitly states that the event server constructs a composite event message for a speculative event, including the changed requirement plus quantitative constraints from related requirements and sends the customized message to the subscriber. This directly and fully exercises the requirement's mandated behavior.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test covers publishing an event to subscribers in real time but contains no assertion that the eventserver identifies related requirements or constraints for impacted executable models.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test describes a user decomposing a requirement and temporary links being established; it does not assert automatic identification of related requirements and constraints by the eventserver for impacted executable models.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test describes a user-driven refine operation and temporary links being established by the user action. It does not assert that the eventserver automatically identifies related requirements and constraints for impacted executable models.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test describes a user-initiated merge event and reassignment of links; it does not assert that the eventserver identifies all related requirements and constraints for impacted executable models.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: The test explicitly asserts that the event server 'identifies the impacted requirements' and finds subscribers; this shows the eventserver discovers impacted requirements. It does not assert identification of 'all related requirements and constraints' nor any polling/collection of quantitative constraints or explicit handling 'for each impacted executable model', so coverage is partial.

Description: RQ133 The eventserver shall identify all related requirements and constraints for each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test explicitly requires identification of 'all additional requirements' linked to the same artifact and retrieval of quantitative constraints (via DOORS) and packaging them into a composite message per subscriber. These actions semantically satisfy the requirement to identify related requirements and constraints for impacted artifacts/subscribers. While the test text does not literally say 'executable model', the per-subscriber/per-artifact behavior maps to identifying related requirements and constraints for impacted models; core behaviors and constraint collection are asserted.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: TC147 focuses on real-time event publication to subscriber manager(s); it does not describe querying the requirements management tool for quantitative values related to additional requirements/constraints.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: TC153 covers adding a new requirement and event message generation; it does not mention retrieving quantitative values from a requirements management tool.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: TC154 concerns decomposing a requirement and associated event messaging; it does not reference querying a requirements management tool for quantitative values.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: TC155 covers user-driven refinement of requirements and generation of refine event messages; it does not mention querying the requirements management tool for quantitative values or retrieving quantitative constraints.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: TC160 describes identification of subscribers and impacted requirements but does not state that the event server queries the requirements management tool to retrieve quantitative values for related requirements.

Description: RQ134 The eventserver shall retrieve quantitative values related to these additional requirements and constraints through querying the requirements management tool.
Reason: TC162 explicitly describes polling DOORS (a requirements management tool) to obtain additional quantitative information and including those quantitative constraints in the composite event message, which directly addresses the core requirement. However the test case description does not exercise or assert boundary conditions, error handling (e.g. RM tool unavailable), authentication/authorization, units/format validation, or aggregation rules; therefore coverage is partial.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: This test concerns the subscriber manager receiving an event notification; it does not assert that the event server constructs a composite speculative message including related requirements and constraint values.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: This test exercises the subscriber manager processing of a speculative event (parsing, injecting values, executing models). It verifies subscriber-side processing, not the event server's construction of a composite speculative message, so it does not cover the requirement.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: This test covers adding a new requirement via a trigger and generating an event message. It does not verify composition of a composite speculative message that includes related requirements and constraint values.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: This test verifies reception and categorization of speculative events (the identification of speculative events is required), but it does not demonstrate the later steps of collecting related requirements or constraint values nor composing the composite speculative message.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: The test verifies that the event server identifies impacted requirements and locates subscribers for those requirements (a prerequisite to composing per-subscriber messages). It does not verify polling for quantitative constraint values, nor the actual composition of a composite speculative message including those constraints.

Description: RQ135 The eventserver shall create a composite speculative message which includes the speculative event plus all related requirements and constraint values for each impacted model.
Reason: The test explicitly exercises construction of a composite event message for a speculative event and states that the server identifies additional related requirements and polls DOORS for quantitative information, then constructs and sends a customized composite message including the changed requirement plus quantitative constraints. This directly implements the core requirement. Marked partial confidence because the test describes the happy path but does not assert error paths, boundary conditions, or completeness checks for "all" impacted models.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test concerns subscriber manager behavior after receiving an event (processing/injecting values/re-executing models) and response generation. It does not assert that the eventserver sends a customized message to each impacted executable model; it instead shows subscriber-side processing, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test covers reception, parsing, categorization, and logging of incoming events. It does not contain assertions that the eventserver constructs customized messages or sends them to each impacted executable model.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test verifies identification of impacted subscribers (a prerequisite) but contains no assertion that the eventserver constructs a customized message per subscriber or that it sends the message to each impacted executable model. It partially supports the requirement by proving the identification step only.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test asserts the eventserver sends a notification to each relevant subscriber manager (per-recipient sending). However, it does not state that the message is customized per impacted executable model, nor that the final recipients are executable models rather than subscriber managers. Thus it partially supports the requirement (delivery to each recipient) but lacks explicit customization and direct delivery to executable models evidence.

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test explicitly describes per-subscriber processing ("For each identified subscriber the event server ...") and states that a composite/customized event message is constructed and sent to the relevant subscriber. Semantically aligns with requirement: eventserver constructs customized message and sends it to each impacted subscriber (interpreted as impacted executable model).

Description: RQ136 The eventserver shall send the customized message to each impacted executable model.
Reason: This test is about a user processing event messages and updating artifacts/links. It does not assert that the eventserver constructs or sends customized messages to impacted executable models.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC161 focuses on notifying subscribers when an evolutionary event occurs (sending notifications). It does not assert that a user can view all events for a specific artifact nor that events are displayed in receipt order.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC162 concerns construction of composite event messages for speculative events and related server-side behavior; it does not assert user-facing display of all events in receipt order for a specific artifact.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC163 explicitly states that event messages are displayed in the order in which they were received for a single artifact and requires at least three events. This directly and fully verifies the requirement's core behavior (view all events for a specific artifact in receipt order).

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC164 demonstrates that events are received for a single subscriber and that the system enforces an ordering for processing (prohibits processing out of order). This implies an ordering of events but does not explicitly assert that the user views all evolutionary events for a specific artifact in receipt order. Thus it provides partial supporting evidence.

Description: RQ137 The user shall view all evolutionary events received for a specific artifact in the order in which they were received.
Reason: TC165 indicates event messages are displayed for a single artifact and that the user examines the first event message, which supports that messages are available and an ordering exists. However it does not explicitly assert that all events are viewable or that the display order is explicitly the order received (no explicit ordering assertion like TC163). Therefore it partially supports the requirement.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: This test validates that an event is published in real time to subscribers but contains no steps or assertions about the user updating artifacts based on the event message.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
