Reason: This test covers adding a new requirement (and sending an event message) but does not assert that a user updates related artifacts in response to received event messages.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: This test verifies receipt, parsing, categorization, and storage of events by the event server; it does not document user-driven updates to related artifacts based on event content.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: This test covers server-side construction of composite event messages and sending them to subscribers, but does not include user actions that update related artifacts in response to received event messages.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: This test verifies display ordering of event messages ('Event messages are displayed in the order in which they were received.') but does not exercise or assert that the user updates related artifacts based on event message content.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.
Reason: The test explicitly instructs the user to apply information from the event message to update the artifact and documents the postcondition that links are changed per the user's directions; this directly exercises the requirement's behavior.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: This test documents creation of tentative links when a requirement is deleted (i.e., temporary trace creation). It does not verify elimination of those temporary traces; thus it does not satisfy the requirement.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: This test covers reception, parsing, categorization and logging of events. It does not include assertions about elimination of temporary traces constructed during initial processing.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: This test verifies identification of impacted requirements and subscribers. It does not assert user-driven elimination or automatic removal of temporary traces.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: This test verifies composition and sending of composite event messages (construction of artifacts), but does not assert removal/elimination of temporary traces.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: This test asserts ordering constraints for event processing and that incorrect-order processing is prohibited. It does not assert removal of temporary traces or user-driven elimination of tentative traces.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: The test explicitly states the user discards tentative links and that any additional event messages sent because of those tentative links are removed from the event processing list and display. This directly exercises elimination of temporary traces (tentative links and derived event messages) constructed during initial processing.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC149 states that a received non-speculative event notification is stored in the local event log. It does not address removal of events when temporary traces are eliminated.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC150 indicates that speculative event notifications are processed according to protocols; it does not assert that events are removed when users eliminate temporary traces/tentative links.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC155 covers establishing temporary links during refinement; it does not verify that events are eliminated when such temporary links are later removed.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC157 describes deletion of a requirement and setting existing links to tentative status; it does not state that events are removed when the user eliminates temporary traces/tentative links.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC164 describes prohibition of out-of-order processing and that no events are processed if user attempts wrong order. It does not assert removal of events when users eliminate temporary traces or tentative links.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC165 postconditions explicitly state that event messages previously sent because of a tentative link that has been eliminated are removed from the event processing list and display. This directly corresponds to the requirement's behaviour of eliminating events that are no longer valid due to elimination of temporary traces/tentative links.
